26d73391-8706-4b49-aaae-7d64868f390a.headline: Global clean energy investment hits record high as businesses accelerate transition
26d73391-8706-4b49-aaae-7d64868f390a.body:
Content overview

Companies worldwide are increasing spending on solar, wind, battery storage, and EV infrastructure, driving strong growth across the renewable sector.

Lower technology costs and supportive government policies are helping businesses reduce long-term energy expenses and improve sustainability credentials.

Investors are responding positively, with renewable-focused firms outperforming traditional energy stocks in several markets.

The shift is also creating new jobs, innovation opportunities, and supply-chain expansion, particularly in Europe and Asia.

36b39178-27a5-4718-85b0-c36e826994f8.headline: Markets brace for inflation and energy shock as geopolitical tensions drive volatility
36b39178-27a5-4718-85b0-c36e826994f8.body:
Content overview

Global financial markets are showing heightened volatility as investors await fresh inflation data and react to rising geopolitical tensions impacting energy supplies.

Oil prices have climbed amid fears of supply disruption, which could push inflation higher in coming months, even if near-term inflation readings appear stable.

Bond yields have risen and equity markets remain sensitive to macro signals, reflecting uncertainty about interest-rate direction and economic growth.

Corporate-specific developments are also influencing sentiment — for example, strong earnings boosted some tech stocks, while manufacturing quality issues in the aerospace sector may delay deliveries and weigh on performance.
